# gigahost_server_ipv4 (Resource)

Orders and manages an extra IPv4 address on a Gigahost server. Layer 3 IPv4 addresses can be moved between servers.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "gigahost_server_ipv4" "l2" {
  srv_id  = gigahost_server.example.srv_id
  ip_type = "l2"
}

resource "gigahost_server_ipv4" "l3" {
  srv_id  = gigahost_server.example.srv_id
  ip_type = "l3"
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `ip_type` (String) IPv4 routing mode to order. Must be `l2` or `l3`. The Gigahost read API reports only `primary`/`extra`, so this is kept from configuration and changing it replaces the resource.
- `srv_id` (String) Server id that currently holds the IPv4 address.

### Read-Only

- `ip_address` (String) The IPv4 address.
- `ip_gateway` (String) Gateway.
- `ip_id` (Number) IP address id.
- `ip_netmask` (String) Netmask.
- `ip_reverse` (String) Current reverse DNS (PTR) for the address.

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
# Extra IPv4 addresses can be imported by server id, IP id, and routing mode.
terraform import gigahost_server_ipv4.example 1001/2002/l3
```
